sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: AI·데이터 시대 대응형 솔루션과 반복수익형 과금 모델 강화로 매출 270억 500만 달러를 기록한 2025-10-31 분기

keyPoints.text: Dell은 클라이언트 디바이스부터 서버·네트워킹·스토리지까지 이어지는 포트폴리오로 AI·데이터 시대 요구를 포괄적으로 받치고, 엣지부터 코어·클라우드까지의 통합 제안으로 ‘필수 기술 파트너’ 포지셔닝을 유지하고 있다.
매출 인식 및 판매 방식은 전통 구매 외에 유틸리티, 구독, as-a-Service, 운영리스, 대출을 결합해 고객이 시간에 따라 결제할 수 있게 하고, 이로써 반복수익형 관계를 강화하려는 방향이 분명하다.
경영진은 AI, 소프트웨어 정의, 클라우드 네이티브 인프라 역량을 기반으로 연구개발을 계속 확대해 신제품과 강화된 솔루션을 시장에 내놓겠다는 사업 추진 의지를 반복적으로 강조한다.

riskChanges.text: 회사 스스로 2025년 1월 31일 종료 연차보고서의 Part I Item 1A 리스크가 영업 실적, 재무 상태, 사업 전망을 실질적으로 훼손할 수 있다고 명시해 과거 공시 리스크가 현재에도 여전히 유효함을 재확인한다.
현재 알려지지 않았거나 현재는 중요하지 않다고 판단한 리스크도 추후에는 실질적이고 중대한 부정적 영향을 줄 수 있다고 경고해, ‘숨은 리스크’ 가능성을 배제하지 않는 메시지를 담고 있다.
또한 향후 진술은 다수의 리스크·불확실성에 노출되어 실제 결과가 다르게 나타날 수 있다고 밝혀, 실적 전망을 지나치게 선결하지 말아야 함을 강조한다.

businessChanges.text: 고객 획득 구조가 전통 구매 중심에서 유연한 결제·소유 모델로 확장되며, Dell Financial Services 계열이 결제와 임대·리스·융자형 조합을 지원해 접근성을 높이는 형태로 정교화되고 있다.
비GAAP 영업비용은 3분기 2%, 9개월 3% 감소했고, 이는 직원 보상 및 복리후생 지출 감소가 주 동력이라고 설명되어 단기 비용 구조 개선이 운영 효율 측면에서 먼저 반영된 상태다.
R&D는 3분기 1%, 9개월 2% 증가해 비용 측면에서는 확대되고 있으며, 신기술·신규 기능 투자 지속을 통해 포트폴리오 확장과 제품차별화에 박차를 가하는 흐름이 확인된다.

financialHighlights.text: 매출은 270억 500만 달러, 영업이익은 21억 1,900만 달러, 순이익은 15억 4,800만 달러로 당기 실적의 손익 중심 축이 분명히 제시됐다.
총자산은 874억 7,900만 달러, 현금및현금성자산은 95억 6,900만 달러로 자본구조와 현금성 자원의 기본 크기를 통해 재무 완충성과 운영 여력을 가늠할 수 있다.

watchItems.text: 반복수익형 과금 구조가 실제로 매출 방어력으로 전환되는지, 그리고 R&D 투자(3분기 1%, 9개월 2% 증가)로 이를 뒷받침할 수 있는지가 향후 추적 포인트다.
데이터·AI 수요가 높다는 점과 글로벌 공급망·신속 대응 역량을 긍정 신호로 보더라도, 미확인 리스크와 예측치 불일치 가능성 경고가 함께 제시되므로 성장속도와 리스크 충격을 병행 모니터링해야 한다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2025-10-31 revenue = 270억 500만 달러 (USD 27.005B; 27,005 million), XBRL range 2025-08-02..2025-10-31.
Quarter ended 2025-10-31 operating income = 21억 1,900만 달러 (USD 2.119B; 2,119 million), XBRL range 2025-08-02..2025-10-31.
Quarter ended 2025-10-31 net income = 15억 4,800만 달러 (USD 1.548B; 1,548 million), XBRL range 2025-08-02..2025-10-31.
Quarter ended 2025-10-31 total assets = 874억 7,900만 달러 (USD 87.479B; 87,479 million), XBRL range instant..2025-10-31.
Quarter ended 2025-10-31 cash and cash equivalents = 95억 6,900만 달러 (USD 9.569B; 9,569 million), XBRL range instant..2025-10-31.

ITEM 1A — RISK FACTORS In addition to the risks and uncertainties set forth in this report, the risks discussed in “Part I — Item 1A — Risk Factors” in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended January 31, 2025 could materially affect our business, operating results, financial condition, or prospects. The risks described in such Annual Report on Form 10-K and our subsequent SEC reports are not the only risks facing us. There are additional risks and uncertainties not currently known to us or that we currently deem to be immaterial that also may materially adversely affect our business, operating results, financial condition, or prospects. <div

Overview Dell Technologies is a leader in the global technology industry focused on providing broad and innovative technology solutions for the data and artificial intelligence (“AI”) era. We build and offer solutions ranging from client devices and peripherals to infrastructure solutions across servers, networking, and storage to meet the evolving needs of our customers and drive better business outcomes. With our extensive portfolio and our commitment to innovation, we offer secure, integrated solutions that extend from the edge to the core to the cloud, and we are at the forefront of AI, software-defined, and cloud native infrastructure solutions. Our vision is to become the most essential technology partner. We intend to realize our vision by executing our strategy of leveraging our strengths to extend our leadership positions and capture new growth. We are organized into two busines

Results of Operations — Business Unit Results” and Note 15 of the Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements included in this report. We offer customers choices in how they acquire our solutions, including traditional purchasing and offerings under the Dell Payment Solutions portfolio. These offerings provide both payment and consumption solutions, including utility, subscription, as-a-Service, leases, and loans, which allow our customers to pay over time and provide them with operational and financial flexibility. Dell Financial Services and its affiliates (“DFS”) support financing solutions and services as part of the portfolio. For additional information about our financing arrangements, see Note 4 of the Notes to the Condensed Consolidated Financial Statements included in this report. Revenue and Consumption Models — We expect that our flexible consumption models will further strengthen our customer relationships and provide a foundation for recurring revenue. We define recurring revenue as revenue recognized that is primarily related to hardware and software maintenance, as well as operating leases, subscription, as-a-Service, and usage-based offerings. Strategic Investments and Acquisitions — As part of our strategy, we will continue to evaluate opportunities for strategic investments through our venture capital investment arm, Dell Technologies Capital, with a focus on emerging technology areas that are relevant to our business and that will complement our existing portfolio of solutions. We continue to see opportunities to create value and grow as we respond to long-term demand for our IT solutions driven by a data- and AI-enabled world. We have demonstrated our ability to adjust to changing market conditions with complementary solutions and innovation across both segments of our business, an agile workforce, and the strength of our global supply chain. As we continue to innovate and modernize our offerings, we believe that Dell Technologies is well-positioned for long-term profitable growth. Net

Research and Development — Research and development (“R&D”) expenses are primarily composed of personnel-related expenses incurred in connection with product development. R&D expenses increased 1% and 2% during the third quarter and first nine months of Fiscal 2026, respectively, principally due to continued support of investments in R&D initiatives. 64 Table of Contents As a percentage of net revenue, R&D expenses for the third quarter of Fiscal 2026 and Fiscal 2025 were 2.8% and 3.1%, respectively, and for the first nine months of Fiscal 2026 and Fiscal 2025 were 2.9% and 3.2%, respectively. We continue to support R&D initiatives to innovate and introduce new and enhanced solutions into the market.
